examine.tree: A sturdy old tree. The birds know its branches better than you do.
examine.bush: A tangle of leaves. Something small rustles within, then thinks better of it.
examine.rock: A weathered grey rock. It has outlasted every king who ever cursed it.
examine.oak: A broad-shouldered oak. Strong wood for those patient enough to fell it.
examine.willow: A weeping willow, trailing its branches like a mourner at the water's edge.
examine.fishing-spot: The water ripples. Something with fins is going about its day down there.
examine.fire: A crackling fire. Warm hands, warm hearts, singed eyebrows if you lean in too close.
examine.range: A cooking range, blackened with the labour of a thousand suppers.
examine.furnace: A roaring furnace. Hot enough to coax metal out of stubborn ore.
examine.anvil: A scarred iron anvil. It has rung out more songs than any minstrel.
examine.altar: A quiet altar. Whispered prayers seem to gather here like dust.
examine.bank-booth: A bank booth. Your worldly goods sleep safely behind it.
examine.poll-booth: A polling booth, where the folk of Eldermoor make their voices heard.
examine.ladder-down: A ladder leading down into the dark. Mind the splinters.
examine.ladder-up: A ladder leading up toward the light. The rungs creak a warning.
examine.door: A plain wooden door. It keeps the weather out and the secrets in.
examine.gate: A creaking gate. It swings on hinges that have forgotten the meaning of oil.
examine.fence: A row of weathered fence posts, holding the line against wandering livestock.
examine.wall: A solid stone wall. Generations of moss have made it their home.
examine.window: A small window, its glass warped and dim with age.
examine.pew: A worn wooden pew, polished smooth by countless faithful backsides.
examine.banner: A hanging banner, its colours faded but its pride undimmed.
examine.candle: A lone candle, holding back the dark one flickering inch at a time.
examine.organ: A grand pipe organ. You can almost hear the hymns sleeping in its pipes.
examine.pond: A still pond. Frogs hold court here on warm evenings.
examine.water: Clear water, cool and uncomplaining. It asks nothing and gives much.
examine.sea: The restless sea, stretching out to where the world simply gives up.
examine.dirt-path: A well-trodden dirt path. Countless boots have worn it into the earth.
examine.grass: Soft green grass. It does not care who walks upon it.
examine.sign: A weathered signpost, pointing the way for those who can still read it.
examine.boat: A modest little boat, bobbing patiently at its mooring.
examine.chicken: A plump chicken, strutting about as though it owns the whole farmyard.
examine.rat: A scrawny rat. It eyes you with the bold contempt of a born survivor.
examine.well: A deep stone well. Drop a coin and the dark swallows your wish whole.
examine.crate: A wooden crate. Whatever it held has long since gone elsewhere.
examine.barrel: A stout wooden barrel. It smells faintly of whatever once sloshed within.
examine.sack: A coarse cloth sack, slumped and lumpy with its quiet cargo.
examine.table: A sturdy table, scarred by years of meals, elbows, and idle daggers.
examine.chair: A simple wooden chair. It has held many a weary traveller.
examine.bed: A humble bed. It promises the one luxury that gold cannot buy: rest.
examine.bookshelf: A dusty bookshelf, sagging beneath the weight of forgotten knowledge.
examine.lectern: A reading lectern, worn where a hundred fingers have followed the words.
examine.sconce: A wall sconce, its flame keeping the shadows politely at bay.
examine.dock: A weathered timber dock, salt-stained and creaking with the tide.
examine.ferrite-sword: A dull grey blade of ferrite, harder-edged than bronze.
examine.ferrite-dagger: A short ferrite blade, quicker than its bronze cousin.
examine.ferrite-shield: A round shield faced with ferrite plate, sturdier than wood and studs.
examine.ferrite-full-helm: A close-fitting ferrite helm with a simple nose guard.
examine.ferrite-body: A riveted ferrite chestpiece, heavier than leather but honest protection.
examine.ferrite-legs: Plated ferrite greaves, stiff but reliable in a scrap.
examine.ferrite-boots: Sturdy boots capped with ferrite at heel and toe.
examine.ferrite-gloves: Gloves reinforced with small ferrite plates across the knuckles.
examine.ferrite-axe: A ferrite hatchet, biting deeper into the island's trees than bronze.
examine.ferrite-pickaxe: A ferrite pick, holding its point against tougher rock than bronze.
examine.ferrite-arrows: A bundle of ferrite-tipped arrows, fletched and ready to loose.
examine.steel-sword: A bright steel blade, well-tempered and properly balanced.
examine.steel-dagger: A narrow steel dagger, light and wickedly sharp.
examine.steel-shield: A polished steel shield, broad enough to turn a heavy blow.
examine.steel-full-helm: A steel helm with a visor, hammered to a smooth shine.
examine.steel-body: A heavy steel cuirass, the workhorse armour of Eldermoor's guard.
examine.steel-legs: Steel greaves, riveted at the knee for ease of movement.
examine.steel-boots: Heavy boots shod in steel, built to last.
examine.steel-axe: A steel-headed axe that bites clean and true.
examine.steel-pickaxe: A steel pickaxe, heavy enough to crack open dense seams.
examine.steel-arrows: A bundle of steel-tipped arrows, heavier and harder-hitting.
examine.blacksteel-sword: A blade blackened in the quench, grim-looking and grimly effective.
examine.blacksteel-dagger: A soot-dark dagger, near-invisible in shadow.
examine.blacksteel-shield: A blackened steel shield, scorched dark by the smith's quench.
examine.blacksteel-full-helm: A dark full helm, its slit visor giving nothing away.
examine.blacksteel-body: A blackened cuirass favoured by Eldermoor's veteran sellswords.
examine.blacksteel-legs: Blackened greaves, scorched dark and slow to rust.
examine.blacksteel-boots: Blackened boots, soot-dark and silent on stone.
examine.blacksteel-gloves: Dark gauntlets, scorched in the quench like the rest of the set.
examine.blacksteel-axe: A blackened axe-head, fearsome to look at and sharper still.
examine.blacksteel-pickaxe: A blackened pickaxe, prized by miners who delve the deeper seams.
examine.blacksteel-arrows: A bundle of blackened arrows, fletched dark to match the head.
examine.duskstone-sword: A blade of pale violet duskstone, light yet startlingly strong.
examine.duskstone-dagger: A duskstone dagger, edge gleaming faintly violet.
examine.duskstone-shield: A duskstone shield, surprisingly light for the protection it gives.
examine.duskstone-full-helm: A duskstone helm, cool to the touch even at midday.
examine.duskstone-body: A duskstone cuirass, prized for being lighter than steel yet stronger.
examine.duskstone-legs: Duskstone greaves, supple despite the plate.
examine.duskstone-boots: Light duskstone boots, quiet and sure-footed.
examine.duskstone-gloves: Duskstone-plated gloves, light enough not to slow a swing.
examine.duskstone-axe: A duskstone-headed axe, lighter in the hand than its size suggests.
examine.duskstone-pickaxe: A duskstone pickaxe, biting deep without tiring the arm.
examine.duskstone-longbow: A tall longbow tipped with duskstone nocks, built for distance.
examine.duskstone-arrows: A bundle of arrows tipped with chips of violet duskstone.
examine.verdigrant-sword: A sword of green-veined verdigrant, prized by Eldermoor's captains.
examine.verdigrant-dagger: A verdigrant dagger with a faint green sheen along the fuller.
examine.verdigrant-shield: A verdigrant shield, its green facets turning aside heavy blows.
examine.verdigrant-full-helm: A verdigrant full helm, faceted like cut stone.
examine.verdigrant-body: A verdigrant cuirass, worn by Eldermoor's elite guard.
examine.verdigrant-boots: Verdigrant-shod boots, tough enough for the deepest delves.
examine.verdigrant-gloves: Verdigrant gauntlets, etched with the captain's mark.
examine.verdigrant-axe: A verdigrant-headed axe, prized by Eldermoor's master woodcutters.
examine.verdigrant-pickaxe: A verdigrant pickaxe, able to crack the densest seams with ease.
examine.verdigrant-arrows: A bundle of arrows tipped with green-veined verdigrant.
examine.sunsteel-sword: A radiant sunsteel blade, said to hold a trace of the forge's heat forever.
examine.sunsteel-dagger: A sunsteel dagger that seems to glow faintly in low light.
examine.sunsteel-shield: A sunsteel shield, radiant gold and famously unyielding.
examine.sunsteel-full-helm: A sunsteel full helm, gleaming like captured sunrise.
examine.sunsteel-body: A sunsteel cuirass, the finest armour smithed in Eldermoor.
examine.sunsteel-legs: Sunsteel greaves, radiant and famously hard to dent.
examine.sunsteel-boots: Sunsteel-shod boots, warm to the touch even on cold stone.
examine.sunsteel-gloves: Sunsteel gauntlets, etched with sunburst filigree.
examine.sunsteel-axe: A sunsteel-headed axe, the pinnacle of Eldermoor's woodcutting tools.
examine.sunsteel-pickaxe: A sunsteel pickaxe, the finest a miner could ask to swing.
examine.sunsteel-arrows: A bundle of radiant sunsteel-tipped arrows, the finest fletching in Eldermoor.
examine.willowbark-shortbow: A shortbow of seasoned willowbark, stronger draw than the plain bow.
examine.ashwood-shortbow: A shortbow of dense ashwood, stiff-limbed and hard-hitting.
examine.sunwood-longbow: A longbow of rare sunwood with a sunsteel-reinforced riser, the finest bow on the isle.
